puzzles.title: Cage Jumping
puzzles.author: wuc
puzzles.ruleset:
Standard sudoku rules apply.
Cells separated by an X must sum to 10.
Digits in cages cannot repeat and must sum to the value shown in the upper left corner of the cage.

puzzles.title: Sums by edge
puzzles.author: gwai77
puzzles.ruleset:
Fill all cells with digits between 1 and 9 so that each digit is exactly once in each row, column and block.
Cells a knight's move away must not contain the same digit.
Within a cage, the digits must add up to the sum shown in the top left-hand corner of the cage. Digits must not repeat within a cage.

puzzles.title: spot on the dot
puzzles.author: 99%Sneaky
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. Digits in cages sum to the small number printed in the top left corner. Digits along arrows sum to the digit in the attached circle. Digits along hollow purple lines form a consecutive set, in any order. Adjacent digits on solid green lines must differ by 5 or more. Digits separated by a white dot are consecutive. For pairs of digits separated by a black dot, one must be double the other.

puzzles.title: Little House on the Gridrie
puzzles.author: Chris Napolitano
puzzles.ruleset: Normal sudoku rules apply. Digits along green lines must differ by at least 5. Digits separated by white dots are consecutive. The digit in the grey square is even. The two digits in the killer cage sum to 15. Antiknight: Digits cannot be a knight's move away from themselves.

puzzles.title: Killer Sudoku
puzzles.author: Kutsumi Hane
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply, i.e., each row, column, and 3x3 box must contain the digits 1-9 exactly once.
Additionally:

Killer cages: Digits in cages must sum to the number in the top-left corner and cannot repeat.

puzzles.title: Lucky Sevens
puzzles.author: addie
puzzles.ruleset:
Standard 9x9 sudoku rules apply.
Antiknight and Antiking: Any two cells separated by a knight's or king’s move in chess cannot contain the same digit.
Nonconsecutive: Any two orthogonally adjacent cells cannot contain consecutive digits.
Killer Cages: Digits in a cage may not repeat and must sum to the number in the top left corner of that cage.

puzzles.title: Stishovite
puzzles.author: bellsita and Wisteria Fall (supervised by XeonRisq)
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply. Each row, column, and region indicated by thick borders in the grid must contain the digits 1 to 9 once each.

*** Additionally, no digit may appear more than once on a particular clue type. ***

• German whispers: Adjacent digits on a green line differ by 5 or more.
• Renban: Purple lines must contain a set of consecutive non-repeating digits in any order.
• Modular lines: Any sequence of 3 adjacent digits along a teal line must contain a complete set of residuals modulo 3, i.e: one from (1,4,7), one from (2,5,8) and one from (3,6,9).
• Parity Lines: Digits along a red line must alternate between odd and even.
• Killer Cages: Digits in a cage sum to the number in the top left if given (basically they are cages).
• Odd/Even: The gray circles contain odd digits. The gray squares contain even digits.
• Black Kropki: Cells separated by a black dot have a 1:2 ratio.
• White Kropki: Cells separated by a white dot must be consecutive.

There are no negative constraints of any kind.

puzzles.title: Ranked Japanese Sum Cages
puzzles.author: Justalilguy
puzzles.ruleset:
Japanese Sums: 
Shade some cells in the grid. Fill the unshaded cells with digits from 1-9 such that there are no repeats in any row or column. A Clue outside shows the sum of the digits in a single group of unshaded cells in that row/column. These groups of unshaded cells appear in the order of the clues shown, with at least one shaded cell separating any two groups.

Only the outlined cells outside the grid contain a Japanese Sums clue, and an outlined cell always contains a non-zero clue. All such cells are given. A ? can be any digit from 0-9. A leading ? cannot be 0.

Ranked Cages: Numbers cannot repeat in a cage and no two cage sums are the same. Cage sums are ranked from 1 to 28 with 1 being the lowest cage sum and 28 being the highest. A shaded cell contributes zero to a cage sum.

An inequality sign points to the lower of the two numbers it connects.

puzzles.title: Sweet sweet symmetry
puzzles.author: Pig_
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal Sudoku rules apply and all variants are standard.

Place the digits 1-9 once each in every row, column and box.

Killer: Digits in a cage cannot repeat and add up to the number in the top-left corner.

Thermo: Along thermometres digits must increase from the bulb.

Arrows: Digits along an arrow sum to the number in the connected circle. Digits may repeat along an arrow if allowed by other rules.

Kropki: Digits seperated by a white dot must be consecutive. Not all white dots are necessarily given. 

X Sums: Digits seperated by an X sum to 10. Not all X's are necessarily given.

puzzles.title: Milk and Cookies
puzzles.author: 99%Sneaky
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply.
Digits in cages sum to the small number in the top left corner.
Adjacent digits along dashed green lines must differ by 5 or more.
Digits along solid purple lines form a consecutive set, in any order.
Each set of three consecutive digits along a hollow peach line will contain one low digit (1,2,3), one medium digit (4,5,6) and one high digit (7,8,9).

puzzles.title: Parity Fun 34
puzzles.author: galium_odoratum
puzzles.ruleset: Parity Cages: Each cage is either an odd cage or an even cage. In an odd cage, the sum of all odd digits equals the total given in the top-left corner. In an even cage, the sum of all even digits equals the total given in the top-left corner. It is up to the solver to determine the type of each cage.

puzzles.title: Stitched Lines
puzzles.author: Will Power
puzzles.ruleset:
Thanks to NicoD's puzzle "Serpentine" as the inspiration for the line pattern.
Normal Sudoku rules apply.
ROTATIONAL SYMMETRY- Cells 180 degrees apart in the puzzle sum to 10.
KILLER CAGE- Numbers in the cage sum to the number in the top left corner of the cage.
GERMAN WHISPERS LINES- Numbers on green lines are at least 5 apart from their neighbors on the line.
RENBAN(+)- Numbers on purple lines are each a set of non-repeating, consecutive numbers, in any order. Also, each RENBAN line contains either a 1 or 9.
KROPKI PAIRS- Numbers with a white dot between them are consecutive. Numbers with a black dot between them are in a ratio of 2:1. Not all possible dots are shown.

puzzles.title: Five Consecutive Knights Straight
puzzles.author: AaronH
puzzles.ruleset:
Normal sudoku rules apply. All variant rules are standard.
- Anti-knight: Cells separated by a knights move in chess cannot contain the same digit. The Anti-knight icon is a reminder for this rule and has no special meaning for the cell it is located in.
- White Kropki: Digits must be consecutive in cells separated by a white dot. Not all dots are given.
- Killer cages: Digits in cages must sum to the value shown in the top corner
- Arrows: Digits along an arrow must sum to the number in the bulb
- Renban: Along pink lines, digits form a consecutive sequence in any order.
